seoTitle: YXC Timing Solutions | Automotive, 5G, IoT Applications | BeiLuo
seoDescription: Complete timing solutions using YXC crystals and oscillators for automotive, 5G communications, and IoT applications. Reference designs, BOM lists, and technical support.

faqs.question: How do I choose the right timing solution for my application?
faqs.answer: Consider your frequency requirements, stability needs, operating environment, and cost constraints. For automotive, use AEC-Q200 qualified parts. For high-speed communications, choose differential oscillators with low jitter. For battery-powered IoT, select low-power 32.768kHz crystals.

faqs.question: Can YXC provide custom timing solutions?
faqs.answer: Yes, YXC offers QuickTurn programmable oscillators for custom frequencies. For high-volume applications, custom crystal specifications can be developed. Contact our technical team for custom solution requirements.

faqs.question: What support is available for timing design?
faqs.answer: YXC provides comprehensive support including application notes, reference designs, layout guides, and direct FAE support. BeiLuo offers additional technical assistance for design-in and troubleshooting.

solutions.title: Automotive Electronics Timing Solution
solutions.titleCn: 汽车电子时钟解决方案
solutions.description: Complete timing solution for automotive applications including infotainment, ADAS, body control, and powertrain systems with AEC-Q200 qualified crystals and oscillators.
solutions.longDescription: YXC automotive timing solutions provide reliable clocking for safety-critical automotive electronics. Our AEC-Q200 qualified products meet the stringent requirements of modern vehicles, from infotainment systems to ADAS and powertrain control. With extended temperature ranges, enhanced shock resistance, and full PPAP documentation, YXC automotive crystals and oscillators deliver the reliability needed for automotive applications. As an authorized distributor, BeiLuo provides technical support, reference designs, and fast delivery for automotive projects.
benefits: AEC-Q200 qualified products ensure automotive reliability standards
Extended temperature range -40°C to +125°C for harsh environments
Complete PPAP documentation for automotive production
Enhanced shock and vibration resistance for vehicle operation
Long-term supply stability and change control
coreAdvantages: Zero defect quality program with full traceability
Low EMI emission for automotive EMC compliance
Competitive pricing vs. traditional automotive suppliers

painPoints.title: Harsh Environment Operation
painPoints.description: Automotive electronics must operate reliably across extreme temperature ranges (-40°C to +125°C) with high vibration and shock resistance.

painPoints.title: Long-term Reliability

faeInsights.summary: For automotive designs, always use AEC-Q200 qualified parts even for non-safety applications. The enhanced reliability and documentation requirements will save significant validation time and reduce field failure risk. Keep crystal traces as short as possible (<20mm) and use guard rings around crystal pads to reduce EMI. Match load capacitance precisely for best accuracy and implement proper ground plane under crystal area.
faeInsights.designLogic: Automotive timing design requires balancing reliability, cost, and performance. Start with AEC-Q200 qualified parts, ensure proper PCB layout for EMI compliance, and validate across the full temperature range early in the design phase.
faeInsights.decisionFramework: 1) Identify temperature grade requirement (Grade 0/1/2), 2) Select package size based on space constraints, 3) Choose active vs passive based on reliability needs, 4) Validate with PPAP documentation, 5) Test across full temperature range

faqs.question: What is the difference between AEC-Q200 Grade 0 and Grade 1?
faqs.answer: Grade 1 covers -40°C to +125°C suitable for most under-hood applications. Grade 0 extends to -50°C to +150°C for extreme environments like near exhaust systems. Grade 0 requires more stringent testing.

faqs.question: Do I need PPAP for every crystal part number?
faqs.answer: Yes, PPAP is required for each unique part number in automotive production. However, YXC maintains qualified automotive products with existing PPAP documentation, reducing lead time for standard parts.

faqs.question: How long does automotive qualification take?
faqs.answer: AEC-Q200 testing typically takes 12-16 weeks. However, YXC maintains qualified automotive products in stock with existing PPAP documentation, enabling immediate use without additional qualification time.

faqs.question: What layout considerations are important for automotive crystals?
faqs.answer: Keep traces short (<20mm), use guard rings around crystal pads, implement solid ground plane under crystal, and keep crystal away from high-voltage or high-current traces for EMI compliance.

faqs.question: Can I use commercial-grade crystals in automotive applications?
faqs.answer: No, commercial-grade crystals do not meet automotive reliability requirements. They lack AEC-Q200 qualification and the documentation required for automotive production.

solutions.title: 5G Communication Base Station Timing
solutions.description: High-performance timing solution for 5G base stations, small cells, and communication infrastructure requiring ultra-low jitter and excellent phase noise performance.
solutions.longDescription: YXC differential oscillators provide the ultra-low jitter and excellent phase noise performance required for 5G base stations and high-speed communications. Our LVDS and LVPECL output oscillators support frequencies up to 210MHz with jitter performance under 0.3ps RMS, meeting the demanding requirements of 5G NR, massive MIMO, and high-speed SerDes applications. As an authorized distributor, BeiLuo offers clock tree design assistance, jitter analysis support, and competitive pricing for telecommunications equipment manufacturers.
benefits: Ultra-low jitter <0.3ps RMS meets 5G NR requirements
Excellent phase noise improves system EVM performance
Differential signaling reduces EMI and crosstalk
Wide frequency range 10-210MHz covers all 5G bands
Cost-effective alternative to premium timing brands
coreAdvantages: Sub-0.3ps RMS jitter for high-speed data converters

painPoints.title: Phase Noise Sensitivity
painPoints.description: Massive MIMO systems are sensitive to phase noise which can degrade beamforming performance.

painPoints.title: Synchronization Accuracy

painPoints.title: High Reliability

products.reason: 156.25MHz with ultra-low jitter for high-speed data
Low phase noise for optimal converter performance
CMOS output for general purpose timing

customerCases.challenge: Needed cost-effective timing solution meeting 5G NR jitter requirements while maintaining excellent phase noise for Massive MIMO performance.
customerCases.solution: Implemented YXC YXO-D series differential oscillators for all high-speed clock requirements with optimized power supply filtering.
results: Phase noise performance matched premium brand alternatives

faeInsights.summary: For 5G applications, always characterize the complete clock chain including buffers and dividers. The YXC differential oscillators provide excellent raw performance, but system-level jitter depends on the entire clock distribution network. Use differential signaling for all high-speed clocks, implement proper power supply filtering with ferrite beads, and keep differential traces matched in length.
faeInsights.designLogic: 5G timing design focuses on minimizing jitter and phase noise. Start with high-quality reference oscillators, use differential signaling throughout, implement proper power filtering, and validate with phase noise and jitter measurements.
faeInsights.decisionFramework: 1) Identify jitter budget for each clock domain, 2) Select oscillator based on phase noise specs, 3) Choose differential vs single-ended based on frequency, 4) Implement proper power filtering, 5) Validate with measurements

faqs.question: What is the relationship between phase noise and jitter?
faqs.answer: Jitter is the time-domain representation of phase noise. Phase noise is measured in frequency domain (dBc/Hz). RMS jitter is calculated by integrating phase noise over a specific bandwidth (typically 12kHz-20MHz).

faqs.question: How do I terminate LVDS outputs properly?
faqs.answer: Place a 100Ω differential termination resistor across the output pair at the receiver. Keep traces short (<2 inches) and matched in length within 5 mils. Use proper differential routing with consistent spacing.

faqs.question: Can I use YXC oscillators for synchronous Ethernet?
faqs.answer: Yes, YXC oscillators with ±25ppm or better stability are suitable for SyncE applications. For Stratum 3 compliance, select ±4.6ppm stability options or use with external PLL.

faqs.question: What power supply filtering is needed for low jitter?
faqs.answer: Use a ferrite bead in series with VDD, followed by 10μF and 0.1μF capacitors to ground. Place the 0.1μF capacitor within 2mm of the oscillator. This filtering maintains performance with noisy supplies.

faqs.question: How do I measure phase noise accurately?
faqs.answer: Use a dedicated phase noise analyzer or spectrum analyzer with phase noise measurement capability. Ensure the oscillator has warmed up for at least 30 minutes. Use low-noise power supplies for measurements.

solutions.title: IoT Ultra-Low Power Timing Solution
solutions.titleCn: 物联网超低功耗时钟解决方案
solutions.description: Ultra-low power timing solution for battery-powered IoT devices including smart meters, sensors, wearables, and LPWAN modules with optimized 32.768kHz crystals.
solutions.longDescription: YXC provides ultra-low power 32.768kHz tuning fork crystals optimized for RTC applications with minimal current consumption. Our compact packages down to 1.6×1.2mm enable space-constrained designs while maintaining excellent aging characteristics. As an authorized distributor, BeiLuo offers power consumption calculators, layout guidance, and technical support for battery-powered IoT designs. Our solutions enable 10+ year battery life in smart meters, environmental sensors, and wearable devices.
benefits: Ultra-low power extends battery life to 10+ years
Compact packages enable small product designs
Low ESR reduces drive power requirements

solution.overview: YXC provides ultra-low power 32.768kHz tuning fork crystals optimized for RTC applications with minimal current consumption. Our compact packages down to 1.6×1.2mm enable space-constrained designs while maintaining excellent aging characteristics.

products.role: Primary RTC crystal for smart meters
products.reason: Optimal balance of size, cost, and performance

systemDiagram.description: IoT device timing architecture with RTC and wireless connectivity

customerCases.challenge: Needed 15+ year battery life with accurate timekeeping for tariff switching and tamper detection in outdoor environments.
customerCases.solution: Implemented YXC-3215-32K768 with optimized load capacitance and low-leakage PCB design.
results: Reduced crystal cost by 20% vs. previous solution

faeInsights.summary: For maximum battery life, carefully match the crystal's load capacitance with the MCU's internal load caps. Even small mismatches can significantly increase current consumption. YXC provides load capacitance customization for high-volume applications. Use minimum required load capacitance to reduce power, implement proper guard ring around 32kHz crystal, and keep crystal far from RF circuits.
faeInsights.designLogic: IoT timing design prioritizes power efficiency. Select crystals with low ESR and appropriate load capacitance, minimize parasitic capacitance with short traces, and use proper layout techniques to prevent leakage currents.
faeInsights.decisionFramework: 1) Calculate power budget for timing components, 2) Select crystal with lowest ESR in required package, 3) Optimize load capacitance for minimal power, 4) Implement low-leakage PCB layout, 5) Validate current consumption in sleep mode

faqs.question: How much current should a 32.768kHz RTC circuit consume?
faqs.answer: Well-designed RTC circuits typically consume 0.3-0.8μA. Poor designs may consume 2-5μA or more. The difference comes from crystal ESR, load capacitance matching, and oscillator circuit design.

faqs.question: Does crystal package size affect power consumption?
faqs.answer: Yes, smaller packages generally have higher ESR which increases drive power. However, the difference is usually small (0.1-0.2μA). For extreme low power, use the smallest package that meets ESR requirements.

faqs.question: Can I reduce power by using smaller load capacitors?
faqs.answer: Yes, but this affects frequency accuracy. Using smaller CL than specified causes the crystal to run faster. For example, using 6pF instead of 12.5pF might save 0.2μA but cause +100ppm frequency error.

faqs.question: What causes RTC current to increase over time?
faqs.answer: Common causes: PCB contamination absorbing moisture, flux residue becoming conductive, corrosion from humidity, capacitor degradation, or crystal aging increasing ESR. Proper cleaning and conformal coating prevent these issues.

faqs.question: How do I calculate battery life for my IoT device?
faqs.answer: Battery life (hours) = Battery capacity (mAh) / Average current (mA). Calculate average current considering sleep mode current (with RTC), active mode current, and duty cycle. Use our online calculator for detailed estimates.
